front: Poem: speaker declares "I myself am hell" while observing an animal that "jabs her wedge-head in a cup of sour cream" and "will not scare"

front: Poem: prose memoir at the address of a childhood home

front: Poem: train journey from Rome to Paris symbolizes dissolution of Catholic faith

Poem: a friend rebuked: "art just isn't worth that much"

front: Author: went to jail as conscientious objector during WWII
Author: descended from Jonathan Edwards and the New England literary family that includes James Russell (A Fable for Critics)
Author: 30-year correspondence with Elizabeth Bishop collected in Words in Air
Author: they were introduced by Randall Jarrell
